title: Natural Solutions: 10 Best Herbal Teas for Dermatitis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dermatitis are infusions derived from various plants that help alleviate the symptoms of skin inflammation and irritation associated with dermatitis. These teas are effective in soothing and calming the skin, reducing redness and itching. The best herbal teas for dermatitis include Calendula officinalis, Urtica dioica, Hypericum perforatum, Symphytum officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Taraxacum officinale, Chamomilla recutita, Aloe vera, Melissa officinalis, and Echinacea purpurea. If left untreated, dermatitis can lead to anxiety, depression, and sleep disturbances, causing immense discomfort and impacting daily life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe and calm skin conditions like dermatitis. This is because the tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling. The tea also has antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections that can make dermatitis worse. Additionally, calendula tea has antioxidants that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. When applied topically or drunk as a tea, the active ingredients in calendula officinalis can help to calm itching and irritation, reduce inflammation, and promote healing of the skin. This can help to improve the appearance and feel of the skin, and reduce the symptoms of dermatitis. Calendula officinalis tea may also help to reduce the risk of scarring and promote healthy skin regeneration.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, making it a natural remedy to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Kaempferol is good for dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce skin redness, swelling, and irritation associated with the condition.
Tannins are good for dermatitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling, soothe itchy skin, and prevent further irritation, providing relief from symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for dermatitis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal tea for dermatitis relief, start by gathering a handful of dried horsetail roots and stems, also known as Equisetum arvense. You can purchase this herb from a health food store or grow it in your garden. Next, steep one teaspoon of dried horsetail in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to bring out its medicinal properties. Then, let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature. Once cooled, you can strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining plant particles. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation associated with dermatitis. You can also add honey or lemon to the tea if desired, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using Equisetum arvense tea as a treatment for dermatitis, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a good remedy for dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink this tea, it helps to reduce redness and swelling on the skin. The anti-inflammatory compounds in aloe barbadensis tea also help to calm the itch and discomfort that often comes with dermatitis. This is because it has a soothing effect on the skin, making it less irritated and more comfortable. Aloe barbadensis tea also has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from further damage and promote healing. This can lead to faster recovery from dermatitis and a decrease in the number of flare-ups. Drinking aloe barbadensis tea regularly may also help to strengthen the skin's natural barrier, making it more resistant to irritation and inflammation.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for dermatitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also promote the healing of damaged skin.
Aloin is good for dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching in the skin.

Flavonoids are good for dermatitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin, making them a natural remedy for relieving skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.

Tannins are good for dermatitis because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling in the skin, making them a useful natural remedy for soothing and calming irritated skin.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for dermatitis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal tea that helps with dermatitis, start by gathering fresh or dried Equisetum arvense plant material. If using fresh, you'll need to dry it first by tying it in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once dried, crush the Equisetum arvense plant material into small pieces to release its active ingredients. Next, combine one tablespoon of the crushed Equisetum arvense with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to help the active compounds infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a separate container to remove the plant material and discard the solids. Finally, drink the Equisetum arvense tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of dermatitis. It's essential to note that while Equisetum arvense tea may provide relief, it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any medical condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, has been used for centuries to help soothe skin problems like dermatitis. This herbal tea is made from the leaves of the stinging nettle plant, which may seem counterintuitive, but the plant's properties make it very beneficial. The leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness and swelling in the skin, which are common symptoms of dermatitis. Urtica dioica tea also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off infections that can trigger dermatitis. When applied topically or consumed as a tea, the antioxidants in nettle tea can help calm the skin and reduce irritation. Additionally, Urtica dioica tea may help to reduce itching and improve skin health, making it a potential natural remedy for people suffering from dermatitis.

Polyphenols are good for dermatitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin conditions.

remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for dermatitis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal tea, which is known to help with dermatitis, you will need fresh or dried horsetail plants, a large pot, and a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth. First, if using fresh horsetail, pick a few stalks and clean them thoroughly with water to remove any dirt. Next, chop the stalks into small pieces and place them in a large pot. If using dried horsetail, simply add the required amount to the pot. Pour in four cups of boiling water over the horsetail, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes, depending on your preference for the tea's strength. Strain the liquid into a cup using a tea infuser or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. Let the tea cool down, then drink it as is, or add honey or lemon to taste. It is recommended to drink 2-3 cups of this herbal tea per day to help alleviate dermatitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St John's Wort, has been used for centuries to help soothe and calm skin irritations, including dermatitis. The tea is made from the flowers of the Hypericum plant, which are rich in active compounds like hyperforin and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dermatitis. When applied topically or consumed as a tea, the flavonoids and hyperforin in Hypericum perforatum can penetrate deep into the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, the tea may also help to improve the skin's natural barrier function, allowing it to repair and regenerate itself more efficiently. As a result, many people find that Hypericum perforatum tea helps to alleviate symptoms of dermatitis, such as dryness, itchiness, and irritation.

Tannins are good for dermatitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also act as an astringent to dry out itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal tea to help with dermatitis, start by gathering fresh or dried horsetail plant, also known as Equisetum arvense. If using fresh horsetail, carefully pick the green stems and leaves, making sure not to damage them. Clean the horsetail thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris. If using dried horsetail, simply use the dried plant material. Next, add one teaspoon of the horsetail plant to a cup of boiling water, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into another cup and discard the solids. Allow the tea to cool down before drinking it. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of the Equisetum arvense herbal tea a day to help alleviate dermatitis symptoms. You can also add honey or lemon to the tea if you prefer a sweeter or tangier taste. Remember to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using Equisetum arvense tea for medicinal purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that may help soothe and calm dermatitis. The plant's roots and flowers contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling in the skin. When consumed as a tea, these compounds may help to calm the immune system's response to irritation, reducing the severity of dermatitis symptoms. The tea may also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Some people believe that Echinacea purpurea tea can help to promote healing and reduce the risk of infection, making it a popular choice for those looking for a natural remedy for dermatitis. Additionally, the tea's soothing and calming effects may help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate dermatitis symptoms. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea regularly may also help to build up the body's natural defenses against skin irritation.

If you have fresh horsetail, dry it first by spreading it out in a single layer on a paper towel and letting it air dry for a few days. Next, place a tablespoon of the dried horsetail in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Pour boiling water over the horsetail, making sure the water level is above the plant. Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, then carefully remove the infuser or muslin bag. Strain the tea into a cup, if necessary, and discard the solids. Finally, drink the tea while it's still warm, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help soothe and calm skin irritations associated with dermatitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tea is a natural remedy that may help with dermatitis. Dermatitis is a skin condition that causes redness, itching, and swelling. The tea, also known as comfrey, is made from the leaves of the comfrey plant. It contains compounds that may reduce inflammation and promote skin healing. Drinking comfrey tea may help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the itching and discomfort associated with dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory properties in comfrey tea may also help to reduce swelling and redness, making it easier to manage symptoms. Some people find that comfrey tea also helps to improve skin tone and texture, leaving skin looking healthier and more radiant. Using comfrey tea as a natural remedy may be especially helpful for people who are sensitive to harsh chemicals or medications often used to treat dermatitis.

First, if using fresh horsetail, wash it gently under cold running water to remove any dirt. Next, chop the stems into smaller pieces, and then add them to a saucepan. If using dried horsetail, just add the required amount to the saucepan. Pour in boiling water, making sure the horsetail is covered, and let it steep for about 5-10 minutes. This will allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, carefully strain the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the horsetail, and then discard the solids. Your Equisetum arvense herbal tea is now ready to drink. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of this tea per day to help soothe and reduce dermatitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tea, also known as dandelion tea, is a natural remedy for skin conditions like dermatitis. The leaves of the dandelion plant contain a number of active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling in the skin, which are common symptoms of dermatitis. The tea also contains vitamins and minerals like vitamin C and potassium that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion tea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate dermatitis. When consumed as a tea, the active compounds in dandelion can also be absorbed into the bloodstream, providing relief from the inside out. Some people have reported that drinking dandelion tea helps to reduce the severity of dermatitis symptoms, such as rashes and blisters, and improves the healing process.

supplementary_best: A combination of Calendula officinalis, Urtica dioica, and Aloe barbadensis teas can help remedy dermatitis.  Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the skin. Its flavonoids and terpenoids can reduce redness and swelling, making it a great addition to the remedy.  Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce itching and prevent infections. Its flavonoids and carotenoids can also help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.  Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin. Its aloin and aloe-emodin can reduce redness and swelling, while its vitamins and minerals can promote skin healing and hydration.  This combination of teas is the best because it provides a balance of soothing, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties that can help to reduce itching, prevent infections, and promote skin healing. The Calendula officinalis can help to reduce redness and swelling, while the Urtica dioica can help to reduce itching and prevent infections. The Aloe barbadensis can help to soothe and calm the skin, while promoting skin healing and hydration.  To make the tea, combine one teaspoon of each herb in one cup of boiling water. Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dermatitis, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. Tea made from goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) is not recommended because it contains berberine, a chemical that can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions.  Ginger (Zingiber officinale) tea may not be suitable for everyone with skin conditions. While it has anti-inflammatory properties, some people may be allergic to ginger or find it too spicy, which can irritate the skin further.  St. John's Wort (Leonurus cardiaca) tea contains hyperforin, a chemical that can cause skin sensitivity and allergic reactions in some people.  Dandelion root (Taraxacum root is not mentioned, but some sources use the root in herbal teas) is not recommended if you have skin issues. The root contains inulin, a chemical that can cause skin irritation in some individuals.  Burdock (Arctium lappa) root tea may cause skin irritation in some people due to its high saponin content, which can cause skin dryness and irritation.  Ephedra (Ma Huang) tea can be problematic for people with skin conditions. It contains ephedrine, a chemical that can cause skin sensitivity and allergic reactions in some individuals.  Tea made from red clover (Trifolium pratense) contains isoflavones, which can cause skin sensitivity and allergic reactions in some people.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help with dermatitis. The gel from the aloe vera plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and redness on the skin. It also has soothing properties that can calm irritated skin and promote healing. Aloe vera gel can be applied directly to the affected skin, usually 2-3 times a day.  Oatmeal baths are another natural remedy that can help with dermatitis. Oatmeal has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can calm and moisturize the skin. Oatmeal baths can be made by adding colloidal oatmeal to warm water and soaking in it for 15-20 minutes. This can help to reduce itching and irritation, and promote healing of the skin.  Coconut oil is a natural moisturizer that can help with dermatitis. The fatty acids in coconut oil can help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin. Coconut oil can be applied directly to the affected skin, usually 2-3 times a day.  Apple cider vinegar is a natural remedy that can help with dermatitis. The acidity in apple cider vinegar can help to balance the pH of the skin and reduce inflammation. Apple cider vinegar can be applied to the affected skin using a cotton ball, usually 1-2 times a day. However, it's recommended to dilute the apple cider vinegar with water first, as undiluted apple cider vinegar can be too harsh on the skin.  Some natural remedies that can help with dermatitis also include taking omega-3 fatty acid supplements, which can help to reduce inflammation, and applying topical probiotics to the affected skin, which can help to promote a healthy skin microbiome.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dermatitis often experience other skin issues like eczema, acne, and psoriasis. This is because dermatitis is a skin condition that can trigger an allergic reaction, leading to inflammation and irritation.  Aloe vera is often used to heal these skin issues. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to manage eczema and other skin conditions. Aloe vera also has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections that can make skin conditions worse.  Chamomile is another medicinal herb that can help to soothe and calm skin irritations. It has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to reduce redness and prevent infections. Chamomile tea or oil can be applied topically to the affected area, or taken internally as a tea to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can exacerbate skin conditions.  Tea tree oil is also effective in managing skin conditions. It has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infections and reduce inflammation. Tea tree oil can be applied topically to the affected area, but it's essential to dilute it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to avoid irritation.  Turmeric is a medicinal herb that contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin can help to reduce inflammation and prevent scarring, making it an effective treatment for skin conditions like acne and eczema. Turmeric can be applied topically as a paste or taken internally as a supplement.  These medicinal herbs can help to manage skin conditions like dermatitis, eczema, acne, and psoriasis by reducing inflammation, preventing infections, and promoting relaxation. They work by targeting the underlying causes of these conditions and providing relief from symptoms.
